name: Verdict record binding with did:web verifier
description: content_hash binds to the tamper-proof verdict record, not to the detected content itself. The receipt at url IS the verdict; the hash proves the verdict has not been altered.
rationale: The receipt contains the verifier's judgment (PASS/FAIL per vector). content_hash proves THIS judgment was issued, not that the underlying subject is correct.

name: Verdict binding with did:aip verifier (enforcement layer)
description: An enforcement verdict (boundary check) issued by a did:aip-identified agent. The verdict record says what boundary was hit; the attestation says the verdict exists and is tamper-proof.
rationale: Enforcement verdicts (rule_type, pass/block) are a valid receipt type. The verifier DID identifies the enforcement agent. content_hash binds to the verdict, not the scanned payload.

name: Verdict binding with coverage and version metadata in receipt
description: The receipt (at url) contains version and coverage fields per enforcement layer design. The attestation envelope does not carry those fields -- they live in the receipt. The envelope only points to the receipt.
rationale: The envelope is intentionally minimal. Coverage fields, version fields, rule_type, detection details all live in the receipt body. The attestation is a pointer with integrity, not a container for enforcement metadata.

name: Composition: attestation verifier + enforcement verifier are different DIDs
description: The attestation was issued by a conformance verifier. The receipt it points to may reference enforcement verdicts from a different DID. The verifier field identifies who issued THIS attestation, not who ran the enforcement.
rationale: The verifier field identifies the attestation issuer. If the receipt references enforcement verdicts from did:aip agents, those are cited INSIDE the receipt, not in the envelope. Clean layer separation.
